# Accessibility Checklist: Search, Accessibility, Deep Links, Docs Validation

**Purpose**: Validate DOC-010 accessibility requirement completeness, clarity, consistency, and measurable review evidence before implementation.
**Created**: 2026-06-19
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are keyboard operation requirements defined for each meaningful `SafeInstallAids.astro` control and result state? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK002 Are keyboard operation requirements defined for `LifecycleFlow.astro` controls or content states without relying on pointer-only interaction? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK003 Does the spec define when `SafeInstallAids.astro` must expose accessible status announcements for changed guidance or copied/selected output? [Resolved, Spec §FR-005, Spec §Accessibility Review Requirements]
- [x] CHK004 Does the spec define a native-control or semantic-control preference for interactive aid controls before custom ARIA patterns are introduced? [Resolved, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-006, Spec §Accessibility Review Requirements]
- [ ] CHK005 Are static fallback requirements documented for both `SafeInstallAids.astro` and `LifecycleFlow.astro` when dynamic behavior is unavailable? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-006, Spec §SC-004]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Are visible-focus expectations stated clearly enough to review on desktop and mobile layouts? [Clarity, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-006, Spec §SC-003]
- [x] CHK007 Are contrast and reflow expectations measurable enough to avoid overlap, clipped labels, or unreadable controls across docs-site routes? [Resolved, Spec §Accessibility Review Requirements, Spec §SC-003, Plan §Phase 0 Research Summary]
- [ ] CHK008 Is non-JavaScript readability defined as preserving equivalent install or lifecycle guidance rather than merely leaving markup on the page? [Clarity, Spec §SC-004]
- [ ] CHK009 Are manual accessibility review boundaries explicit enough to avoid claiming automation alone proves full accessibility compliance? [Clarity, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Phase 0 Research Summary]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK010 Do `SafeInstallAids.astro` and `LifecycleFlow.astro` accessibility requirements use consistent terminology for keyboard access, focus visibility, labels, contrast, fallback content, and responsive behavior? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-006]
- [ ] CHK011 Do browser smoke requirements align with the spec assumption that Playwright samples representative deep links and does not certify full accessibility? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010, Spec §Assumptions, Contract §Browser Smoke]
- [ ] CHK012 Do PR packet evidence requirements align with the quickstart manual evidence checklist for keyboard, screen-reader-oriented, responsive, fallback, and known-gap notes? [Consistency, Spec §PR Review Packet Requirements, Quickstart §Manual Evidence]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK013 Can keyboard-only review success be objectively judged for primary interactive aid actions without requiring pointer input? [Measurability, Spec §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK014 Can static fallback success be objectively judged for both interactive aids when dynamic behavior is unavailable or bypassed? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004]
- [ ] CHK015 Can browser smoke evidence be reviewed without expanding into a broad visual snapshot or full accessibility certification suite? [Measurability, Spec §SC-006, Contract §Browser Smoke]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK016 Are narrow viewport, disabled-dynamic-behavior, keyboard-only, screen-reader-oriented, and manual-review scenarios all addressed in the spec or plan? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK017 Are known accessibility gaps or deferred work required to be recorded in reviewer-visible PR packet sections? [Coverage, Spec §PR Review Packet Requirements]
- [ ] CHK018 Are forbidden validation actions documented so accessibility review does not depend on networked installs, local user files, destructive commands, or browser-side local command execution? [Coverage, Spec §FR-011, Quickstart §Forbidden In Automated Validation]

## Notes

- Focus areas selected: keyboard operation, focus visibility, native controls, status announcements, static fallback, non-JavaScript readability, responsive reflow, contrast, and manual/browser evidence.
- Depth level: Standard reviewer gate.
- Actor/timing: PR reviewer before implementation tasks begin.

## Re-run Verification

- [x] CHK019 Are status-announcement requirements now explicit for `SafeInstallAids.astro` selector and copy-result changes? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005, Spec §Accessibility Review Requirements]
- [x] CHK020 Are native-control and semantic-HTML preferences now explicit for `SafeInstallAids.astro` and `LifecycleFlow.astro`? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-006, Spec §Accessibility Review Requirements]
- [x] CHK021 Are contrast, visible-focus, and reflow expectations now measurable while preserving the no-certification boundary? [Clarity, Spec §Accessibility Review Requirements, Plan §Phase 0 Research Summary]
